$schema: informal — see docs/meta/roadmap.md for how this file is used

phase1.description: Core coverage: onboarding, the 10 high-confidence scenerystack/* libraries, and the non-API categories. Fully enumerated — each entry is a concrete file to create. A future agent claims one by creating docs/<path> with status: stub and filling it in over time.

pages.title: Installation and Setup
pages.description: Setting up a new SceneryStack project: npm install, TypeScript config, and dev tooling.

pages.title: Your First Simulation
pages.description: Scaffolding a minimal one-screen simulation with Sim, Screen, and ScreenView.

pages.title: Scenery Application vs. Standalone Library
pages.description: Choosing between a full joist-based Sim application and using scenery as a standalone rendering library.

pages.title: Project Structure Conventions
pages.description: Standard folder/file layout of a SceneryStack repo (model/view split, strings, images).

pages.title: Running and Building a Simulation
pages.description: Dev server workflow and production build output for a SceneryStack sim.

pages.title: Supported Browsers
pages.description: Browser/platform support matrix and graceful-degradation considerations.

pages.title: Scenery Basics
pages.description: Introduction to the scene graph: the Node tree, render layers, and coordinate frames.

pages.title: Scenery Layout
pages.description: Overview of scenery's auto-layout system: FlowBox, GridBox, AlignBox, and layout options.

pages.title: Scenery Input
pages.description: Overview of scenery's input system: pointers, listeners, and event phases.

pages.title: UI Components Overview
pages.description: Tour of the sun and scenery-phet component libraries and when to reach for each.

pages.title: Translation and Localization
pages.description: String management with StringProperty and the PhET translation pipeline.

pages.title: Modifying SceneryStack
pages.description: Workflow for patching or contributing to the underlying common-code libraries.

pages.title: PhET-iO and Instrumentation
pages.description: Conceptual guide to PhET-iO: what instrumentation buys you and how Tandem fits in.

pages.title: Building Your First Screen
pages.description: Step-by-step model/view/Screen wiring for a new screen in a multi-screen sim.

pages.title: Accessible Interaction Tutorial
pages.description: Making a custom draggable Node fully accessible end-to-end.

pages.title: Preferences and Feature Flags
pages.description: Using the Preferences dialog and query parameters to configure sim behavior.

pages.title: Property
pages.description: The base observable value wrapper underlying all reactive state.

pages.title: BooleanProperty
pages.description: A Property<boolean> with a toggle() convenience method.

pages.title: NumberProperty
pages.description: A Property<number> with an optional range for validation and UI binding.

pages.title: StringProperty
pages.description: A Property<string>'s role in localizable, translatable values.

pages.title: EnumerationProperty
pages.description: A Property restricted to the values of an EnumerationValue subclass.

pages.title: DerivedProperty
pages.description: A read-only Property computed from one or more dependencies.

pages.title: Multilink
pages.description: Reacting to several Properties at once without manually chaining links.

pages.title: Emitter
pages.description: Discrete events that are not state.

pages.title: Observable Array
pages.description: createObservableArray: a Property-like array with add/remove item emitters.

pages.title: Vector2
pages.description: The 2D vector/point class used throughout SceneryStack's math and positioning APIs.

pages.title: Vector3
pages.description: The 3D vector class used for color math and 3D-flavored simulations.

pages.title: Bounds2
pages.description: An axis-aligned rectangle used for layout, drag bounds, and hit-testing.

pages.title: Matrix3
pages.description: The 3x3 transform matrix behind Node transforms and ModelViewTransform2.

pages.title: Range
pages.description: A min/max pair used for NumberProperty validation and slider ranges.

pages.title: Utils
pages.description: dot's numeric helper functions: clamp, linear, roundSymmetric, and friends.

pages.title: Shape
pages.description: kite's path-description class behind Path, and its common construction/query methods.

pages.title: Node
pages.description: The base class of every item in the scene graph and its core options.

pages.title: Path
pages.description: A Node that renders a kite Shape with fill/stroke.

pages.title: Circle
pages.description: A Path subclass for drawing circles by radius.

pages.title: Rectangle
pages.description: A Path subclass for drawing (optionally rounded) rectangles.

pages.title: Line
pages.description: A Path subclass for drawing single line segments.

pages.title: Text
pages.description: A Node that renders a single string of plain text.

pages.title: RichText
pages.description: A Text-like Node supporting a constrained markup subset for mixed styling.

pages.title: Image
pages.description: A Node that renders a raster image with optional Property-driven swapping.

pages.title: Display
pages.description: The root object that turns a Node tree into rendered/attached DOM output.

pages.title: PressListener and FireListener
pages.description: The base classes behind clickable/pressable Nodes.

pages.title: FlowBox
pages.description: A single-axis (row/column) auto-layout container.

pages.title: GridBox
pages.description: A two-dimensional grid auto-layout container.

pages.title: AlignBox
pages.description: A single-child container for alignment and fixed-size placement within layouts.

pages.title: RectangularPushButton
pages.description: The standard rectangular momentary-action button.

pages.title: TextPushButton
pages.description: A push button whose content is a text label.

pages.title: Toggle Buttons
pages.description: The rectangular/round toggle-button family bound to a BooleanProperty or two-state value.

pages.title: HSlider
pages.description: The horizontal slider bound to a NumberProperty and Range.

pages.title: Checkbox
pages.description: A checkbox bound to a BooleanProperty, with label-Node composition.

pages.title: ComboBox
pages.description: A dropdown selector bound to a Property over an enumerated set of items.

pages.title: Radio Button Groups
pages.description: Mutually-exclusive selection bound to a Property (rectangular and Aqua styles).

pages.title: Panel
pages.description: A bordered/background container used to group related controls.

pages.title: AccordionBox
pages.description: An expandable/collapsible titled container bound to an expandedProperty.

pages.title: ArrowNode
pages.description: A configurable arrow shape used for vectors, force diagrams, and callouts.

pages.title: NumberControl
pages.description: A composite slider + label + value-readout control bound to a NumberProperty.

pages.title: PhetFont
pages.description: The standard font class used across every SceneryStack simulation.

pages.title: ThermometerNode
pages.description: A fluid-level thermometer visualization bound to a temperature Property.

pages.title: ResetAllButton
pages.description: The standard circular reset control wired to a model's reset() method.

pages.title: TimeControlNode
pages.description: The standard play/pause/step-forward control for time-based simulations.

pages.title: StopwatchNode
pages.description: A draggable digital stopwatch bound to a Stopwatch model element.

pages.title: Animation and Easing
pages.description: Animating a Property's value smoothly over time instead of setting it instantly.

pages.title: Sim
pages.description: The top-level application object owning Screens, the navigation bar, and the sim loop.

pages.title: Screen
pages.description: Pairing a model factory and view factory into one selectable unit of a Sim.

pages.title: ScreenView
pages.description: The root Node of a Screen's view, with layoutBounds and visibleBoundsProperty.

pages.title: Tandem
pages.description: The naming/instrumentation handle giving every PhET-iO-tracked element a stable tree address.

pages.title: Emitter vs. Property
pages.description: When to model state as a Property versus a discrete event as an Emitter.

pages.title: The Multilink Pattern
pages.description: Combining multiple Properties into one derived reaction.

pages.title: Dispose and Memory Management
pages.description: The dispose() convention: when Nodes/Properties/listeners must clean up.

pages.title: The Options Pattern
pages.description: The options-object convention for configuring Nodes and components.

pages.title: The Enumeration Pattern
pages.description: Modeling closed sets of values instead of string literals.

pages.title: The Reset-All Pattern
pages.description: Wiring every model Property's reset() through one ResetAllButton.

pages.title: Multi-Screen Sim Structure
pages.description: Structuring model/view code across multiple Screens in one Sim.

pages.title: Query Parameters Pattern
pages.description: Using query parameters to drive optional/debug behavior.

pages.title: PhET-iO Instrumentation Pattern
pages.description: Deciding what to instrument with Tandem and structuring a PhET-iO-ready model.

pages.title: Typography and Fonts
pages.description: Using PhetFont consistently and font-size/weight conventions.

pages.title: Layout Container Conventions
pages.description: Styling conventions when composing FlowBox/GridBox/AlignBox layouts.

pages.title: Panels and Backgrounds
pages.description: Conventions for grouping controls with Panel/AccordionBox.

pages.title: Iconography Conventions
pages.description: Building consistent icons for buttons and controls.

pages.title: Stroke and Line Styling
pages.description: Conventions for stroke width, dash patterns, and line joins/caps.

pages.title: Keyboard Input and Hotkeys
pages.description: KeyboardListener and Hotkey for custom keyboard shortcuts beyond dragging.

pages.title: Alternative Input Overview
pages.description: How pointer, keyboard, and switch/AT input all route through the same listeners.

pages.title: Focus Highlights
pages.description: Customizing focus highlights for focusable Nodes.

pages.title: Voicing
pages.description: Speech-synthesis descriptions triggered by interaction, layered on the PDOM.

pages.title: Describing Dynamic State
pages.description: Writing description content that updates as model state changes.

pages.title: Sound Design
pages.description: Using scenery-phet/tambo sound generators for non-speech audio feedback.

pages.title: Demo Simulation Walkthrough
pages.description: Annotated tour of a minimal end-to-end demo sim.

pages.title: Scenery Layout Examples
pages.description: Worked FlowBox/GridBox layouts for common UI arrangements.

pages.title: Building a Two-Screen Simulation
pages.description: Full example wiring two Screens with shared and per-screen model code.

pages.title: Three.js Integration
pages.description: Embedding a Three.js WebGL scene inside a scenery Node.

pages.title: Standalone Scenery App Example
pages.description: Using scenery as a standalone rendering library outside the joist shell.

pages.title: Accessible Control Panel Example
pages.description: A fully accessible control panel combining sun components with PDOM.

pages.title: PhET-iO Instrumented Sim Example
pages.description: A minimal simulation instrumented end-to-end with Tandem.

phase2.description: Deep coverage. Represented as per-library/category targets plus candidate names rather than a rigid fixed list, since exact titles should flex as agents verify real APIs. Do NOT create a page from a 'medium' or 'low' confidence candidate without first confirming the class actually exists and matches this shape (check https://scenerystack.org/reference/ or introspect the installed package) — mark such pages status: draft with a sourceRefs entry until an independent verified pass confirms them.

targets.note: Subpath confirmed to exist (npm registry exports map); exact class names not independently verified — check real exports before writing.

Subpath confirmed to exist; exact class names not independently verified — check real exports before writing.

Pairs with patterns/query-parameters-pattern.md; verify exact API before writing.
